The second thing that happens is everybody refers to him because he is obligated assault. Now, I would refer to him anyway under certain circumstances because he does a great job and I've sent clients to him. But I will tell you, I am more prone to refer to him than I would be without the dinner. And I don't even go over a year. But that kind of makes me think more during the year. I'm almost looking for an opportunity to refer somebody to him to sort of discharge this obligation. Because there is really no other way to discharge the obligation. So I don't know what the number is. I can tell you, in my case, I probably send them three or four good clients a year. Without the dinner, I might send them one. If that's the multiple across the board, he's got all the clients he could handle for a decade. He does no other marketing. I don't think he's got a brochure or he sends out a little packet of testimonial letters and stuff. Certainly, marketing is not very sophisticated. He does nothing else to get business but this. Now, this thing, he writes a big check for this. I mean, this is a big hotel dinner and there's champagne on the table and there's five courses and there's 300 people. I mean, you've written a sizable check. But it's the only check he's ever got to write all year long. By putting people together, he obligates both sides of that equation. And everything that turns out well, of course, we all feel, gee, he connected me with this guy. Gee, he connected me with this guy. I got to connect him with somebody. The message is implicit. It's never expressed. But it's there. I'm connecting you with people. You should be connecting me with people. And he puts himself in the center of all these people who now have the ability and the obligation to connect him with people. It's really a spectacular strategy that I have rarely seen replicated but certainly is replicatable by anybody in a niche. It's replicatable in a community. And really, who gets invited to a big, special VIP, meet important people, you know, kind of dinner, even go to go. And by the way, the formal thing is very smart. Let me tell you why the formal thing is very smart. Because the women all like to dress up in formal gowns and get to wear them somewhere. So they're like pushing. Let's go, let's go, let's go, let's go. On two occasions I've gone more because Carla wanted to go. I mean, I'd have got out of it. And quite frankly, I don't think she'd want to go if it wasn't for the fact that everybody was dressing up in formal gowns. So it's a very smart thing on his part. Also all guys look better in tuxes. So, I mean, an ugly guy looks better in a tux. So you automatically improve the appearance of the crowd just by putting them in tuxes. So I've watched him, this is many years. And I understood what he was doing the minute I saw it. And I thought it was brilliant and I thought it was replicable. Mark McCormick, if you don't know the name, Mark built the first sports management agency. It's called International Management Group, IMG. And prior to him, athletes had, they had a manager, they had an agent, they had a lawyer, they had a CPA, they had a money manager, they had money. And so he took it all. And we're going to put it all under one roof and we're going to do it all for them and essentially we're going to get all their money. The breakthrough for Mark was Arnold Palmer, much like the breakthrough for Peter was Ronald Reagan. The breakthrough was Palmer. Palmer was the first client, markhead of any name whatsoever. But as soon as you had Palmer in 1968, 69, 70, something like that, now everybody wanted to be where Arnold Palmer was. And as soon as you could talk about Arnold Palmer, you could get anybody and everybody to pay attention to you. Bill and Steve Harrison published radio TV interview report, book marketing update, so forth, as with the other day. Their two greatest assets are two clients, Mark Victor Hansen and Robert Kiyosaki. Because if you hold up Mark Victor Hansen and Robert Kiyosaki in front of a bunch of authors, they're like rabbit dogs. They want to be where those guys are. So the strategy is who can I put myself with? Who can I get as a client? Who can I somehow connect myself with who already has reputation that is magnetic to the constituency that I am trying to attract?
